direct_dependencies.title: Complex Class Notation
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: The notation $[a,b]_{\mathbb{C}}$ denotes the \(\sim_{\mathbb{C}}\)-equivalence class of the prepair $(a,b)$.

direct_dependencies.title: Complex Numbers
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: The complex numbers are the quotient \[ \mathbb{C}:=\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{C}/{\sim_{\mathbb{C}}}. \]

direct_dependencies.title: The Real Numbers
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: The real numbers are the set \[ \mathbb{R}:=\{\alpha:\alpha\text{ is a Dedekind cut in }\mathbb{Q}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Field Addition Law
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$, \[ a<b \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+c<b+c. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Field Multiplication Law
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$, \[ c>0 \Longrightarrow \bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow a<b\bigr), \] and \[ c<0 \Longrightarrow \bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow b<a\bigr). \] Moreover, positive elements are closed under multiplication: \[ 0<a \land 0<b \Longrightarrow 0<ab. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Field Trichotomy
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$ and $b$, exactly one of the following holds: \[ a=b,\qquad a<b,\qquad b<a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Distinguished Element Lies in the Underlying Set
Distinguished Element Is Not a Successor
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \neq 1$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Axiom
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A \subseteq P$. If $1 \in A$ and if \[ \forall n \in P\;(n \in A \Longrightarrow S(n) \in A), \] then $A=P$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Closure Under Successor
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: If $n \in P$, then $S(n) \in P$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Injectivity of Successor
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: If $m,n \in P$ and $S(m)=S(n)$, then $m=n$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplicative Cancellation on $\mathbb{Z}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y,z\in\mathbb{Z}$, \[ z\ne 0 \land z\cdot x=z\cdot y \Longrightarrow x=y. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Negation Gives a Right Additive Inverse on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $x\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x+(-x)=0_{\mathbb{Q}}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Negation Gives a Right Additive Inverse on $\mathbb{Z}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $x\in\mathbb{Z}$, \[ x+(-x)=0_{\mathbb{Z}}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One Is Positive in $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In $\mathbb{Q}$, \[ 0_{\mathbb{Q}}<1_{\mathbb{Q}}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One Is a Right Multiplicative Identity on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $x\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x\cdot 1_{\mathbb{Q}}=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One Is a Right Multiplicative Identity on $\mathbb{Z}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $x\in\mathbb{Z}$, \[ x\cdot 1_{\mathbb{Z}}=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Reciprocal Gives a Right Multiplicative Inverse on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every nonzero $x\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x\cdot x^{-1}=1_{\mathbb{Q}}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Addition Preserves Order on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y,z\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x\le y \Longrightarrow x+z\le y+z. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Addition Preserves Strict Order on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y,z\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x<y \Longrightarrow x+z<y+z. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Distributivity on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y,z\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ (y+z)\cdot x=y\cdot x+z\cdot x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Distributivity on $\mathbb{Z}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y,z\in\mathbb{Z}$, \[ (y+z)\cdot x=y\cdot x+z\cdot x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Multiplication by Positives Preserves Order on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y,z\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ 0_{\mathbb{Q}}<z\land x\le y \Longrightarrow x\cdot z\le y\cdot z. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Multiplication by Positives Preserves Strict Order on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y,z\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ 0_{\mathbb{Q}}<z\land x<y \Longrightarrow x\cdot z<y\cdot z. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Sign Rule on $\mathbb{Z}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y\in\mathbb{Z}$, \[ (-x)\cdot y=-(x\cdot y) \qquad\text{and}\qquad x\cdot(-y)=-(x\cdot y). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Zero Is a Right Additive Identity on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $x\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x+0_{\mathbb{Q}}=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Zero Is a Right Additive Identity on $\mathbb{Z}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $x\in\mathbb{Z}$, \[ x+0_{\mathbb{Z}}=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Abelian Group
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{abelian group} is a group whose operation is commutative.

transitive_dependencies.title: Field
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{field} is a commutative ring in which every nonzero element has a multiplicative inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Field
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered field} is a field equipped with a total order compatible with addition and multiplication by positive elements.

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{Q}$-Classes
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define addition on representatives by \[ [a,b]+[c,d]:=[a\cdot d+b\cdot c,\ b\cdot d]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define $+_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0+_{\mathbb{W}}a=a,\qquad a+_{\mathbb{W}}0=a, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=a+_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{Z}$-Classes
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define addition on representatives by \[ [a,b]+[c,d]:=[a+c,b+d]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Associativity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,z,\ x\mathbin{O}(y\mathbin{O}z)=(x\mathbin{O}y)\mathbin{O}z. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Closure
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of $K$ produces an element of $K$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Commutative Monoid
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{commutative monoid} is a monoid whose operation is commutative.

transitive_dependencies.title: Commutative Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{commutative ring} is a ring whose multiplication is commutative.

transitive_dependencies.title: Commutativity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,y,\ x\mathbin{O}y=y\mathbin{O}x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Complex Class Notation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The notation $[a,b]_{\mathbb{C}}$ denotes the \(\sim_{\mathbb{C}}\)-equivalence class of the prepair $(a,b)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Complex Coordinate Equivalence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For $(a,b),(c,d)\in\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{C}$, define \[ (a,b)\sim_{\mathbb{C}}(c,d) \quad:\Longleftrightarrow\quad a=c\ \text{ and }\ b=d. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Complex Numbers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The complex numbers are the quotient \[ \mathbb{C}:=\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{C}/{\sim_{\mathbb{C}}}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Complex Prepair Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The complex prepair set is \[ \operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{C}:=\mathbb{R}\times\mathbb{R}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Dedekind Cut
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{Dedekind cut} is a set $\alpha\subseteq\mathbb{Q}$ satisfying: \[ \alpha\ne\emptyset,\qquad \alpha\ne\mathbb{Q}, \] \[ p\in\alpha\land q

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $R$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. For $a \in A$, the \emph{equivalence class} of $a$ is: \[ [a]_R \;:=\; \{\, x \in A \mid (a,x) \in R \,\}. \] When $R$ is clear from context, we write $[a]$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ be a set. A relation $\sim$ on $A$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Formal-Difference Equivalence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For $(a,b),(c,d)\in\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}$, define \[ (a,b)\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}(c,d) \quad:\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+d=c+b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Fraction Equivalence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For $(a,b),(c,d)\in\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Q}$, define \[ (a,b)\sim_{\mathbb{Q}}(c,d) \quad:\Longleftrightarrow\quad a\cdot d=b\cdot c. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

transitive_dependencies.title: General Recursive Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. A function $f:P\to W$ is called the \emph{general recursive function determined by $(W,c,\Phi)$} exactly when \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Group
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{group} is a monoid in which every element has a two-sided inverse.

transitive_dependencies.title: Integer Class Notation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The notation $[a,b]$ denotes the $\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}$-equivalence class of the prepair $(a,b)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Integer Prepair Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The integer prepair set is \[ \operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}:=\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Integers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The integers are the quotient \[ \mathbb{Z}:=\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}/{\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Data
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $g:W\to W$ be a function. The triple $(W,c,g)$ is called \emph{iterator data} for $(P,S,1)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. A relation $R\subseteq P\times W$ is called an \emph{iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ exactly when \[ (1,c)\in R \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;\forall w\in W\; \bigl((n,w)\in R\Longrightarrow (S(n),g(w))\in R\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ e_L\mathbin{O}x=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x'$ is a left inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x'\mathbin{O}x=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Less Than or Equal on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $a,b \in P$. One writes \[ a \le b \] exactly when \[ a<b \qquad\text{or}\qquad a=b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. The \emph{minimal iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ is \[ R^* = \bigcap\{R\subseteq P\times W : R \text{ is an iterator relation for } (W,c,g)\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Monoid
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ g_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ g_m(1)=m \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(g_m(S(n))=g_m(n)+m). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m \cdot n \coloneqq g_m(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{Q}$-Classes
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define multiplication on representatives by \[ [a,b]\cdot[c,d]:=[a\cdot c,\ b\cdot d]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define $\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}a=0,\qquad a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}0=0, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}b=a\cdot_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{Z}$-Classes
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define multiplication on representatives by \[ [a,b]\cdot[c,d]:=[ac+bd,ad+bc]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Negation on $\mathbb{Q}$-Classes
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define negation on representatives by \[ -[a,b]:=[-a,b]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Negation on $\mathbb{Z}$-Classes
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define negation on representatives by \[ -[a,b]:=[b,a]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Nonzero Integers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The set of nonzero integers is \[ \mathbb{Z}^{\ast}:=\mathbb{Z}\setminus\{0_{\mathbb{Z}}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One in $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define \[ 1_{\mathbb{Q}}:=[1_{\mathbb{Z}},1_{\mathbb{Z}}]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One in $\mathbb{Z}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define \[ 1_{\mathbb{Z}}:=[1,0]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Order on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For $x,y\in\mathbb{Q}$, define \[ x\le y \quad:\Longleftrightarrow\quad x<y \text{ or } x=y. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Order on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define $\leq_{\mathbb{W}}$ on $\mathbb{W}$ by declaring \[ 0\leq_{\mathbb{W}}a\quad(a\in\mathbb{W}), \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a\leq_{\mathbb{W}}b \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a\leq_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Order on $\mathbb{Z}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For $x,y\in\mathbb{Z}$, define \[ x\le y \quad:\Longleftrightarrow\quad x<y \text{ or } x=y. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Partial Order
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A triple $(P,S,1)$ is called a \emph{Peano system} exactly when $P$ is a set, $1 \in P$, $S : P \to P$ is a function, and the following axioms hold: \begin{enumerate} \item[\textbf{P1.}] $1 \in P$. \item[\textbf{P2.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \in P$. \item[\textbf{P3.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \neq 1$. \item[\textbf{P4.}] For all $m,n \in P$, if $S(m)=...

transitive_dependencies.title: Positive Integer
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An integer $x\in\mathbb{Z}$ is \emph{positive} if $x=[a,b]$ for some $a,b\in\mathbb{W}$ with $b<a$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Positive Rational
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A rational number $[a,b]\in\mathbb{Q}$ is \emph{positive} if \[ a\cdot b>0_{\mathbb{Z}}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Prefraction
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{prefraction} is an ordered pair $(a,b)\in\mathbb{Z}\times\mathbb{Z}^{\ast}$, read as the formal quotient $a/b$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Prefraction Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The prefraction set is \[ \operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Q}:=\mathbb{Z}\times\mathbb{Z}^{\ast}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Quotient Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Rational Class Notation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The notation $[a,b]$ denotes the $\sim_{\mathbb{Q}}$-equivalence class of the prefraction $(a,b)$, where $b\ne 0_{\mathbb{Z}}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Rational Numbers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The rational numbers are the quotient \[ \mathbb{Q}:=\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Q}/{\sim_{\mathbb{Q}}}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: The Real Numbers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The real numbers are the set \[ \mathbb{R}:=\{\alpha:\alpha\text{ is a Dedekind cut in }\mathbb{Q}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Reciprocal on Nonzero $\mathbb{Q}$-Classes
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For $[a,b]\ne 0_{\mathbb{Q}}$, define \[ [a,b]^{-1}:=[b,a]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ \forall x,\ x\mathbin{O}e_R=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $x''$ is a right inverse of $x$ with respect to identity $e$ if \[ x\mathbin{O}x''=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes over addition.

transitive_dependencies.title: Semigroup
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Stage-Dependent Step Rule
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system and let $W$ be a set. A \emph{stage-dependent step rule} on $W$ over $P$ is a function \[ \Phi:P\times W\to W. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Strict Less Than on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $a,b \in P$. One writes \[ a<b \] exactly when there exists $c \in P$ such that \[ b=a+c. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Strict Order on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For $x,y\in\mathbb{Q}$, define \[ x<y \quad:\Longleftrightarrow\quad y-x \text{ is positive}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Strict Order on $\mathbb{Z}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For $x,y\in\mathbb{Z}$, define \[ x<y \quad:\Longleftrightarrow\quad y-x \text{ is positive}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Subtraction on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For $x,y\in\mathbb{Q}$, define \[ x-y:=x+(-y). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Subtraction on $\mathbb{Z}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For $x,y\in\mathbb{Z}$, define \[ x-y:=x+(-y). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Total Order
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{total order} on $A$ if it is a partial order and is total.

transitive_dependencies.title: Total (Connex) Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is \emph{total} (or \emph{connex}) if every pair is comparable: \[ \forall a,b \in A, \; (a,b) \in R \lor (b,a) \in R. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Identity
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $e$ is a \emph{two-sided identity} for $O$ if it is both a left identity and a right identity.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two-Sided Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An element $y$ is a two-sided inverse of $x$ if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=e \quad\text{and}\quad x\mathbin{O}y=e. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Whole Numbers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\mathbb{N}$ be the one-based natural-number system developed in the preceding chapter. Let $0$ be a new element with $0\notin\mathbb{N}$. Define \[ \mathbb{W}\coloneqq \mathbb{N}\cup\{0\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Zero in $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define \[ 0_{\mathbb{Q}}:=[0_{\mathbb{Z}},1_{\mathbb{Z}}]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Zero in $\mathbb{Z}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define \[ 0_{\mathbb{Z}}:=[0,0]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Respects Fraction Equivalence on the Left
Addition Respects Fraction Equivalence on the Right
Addition Respects Formal-Difference Equivalence on the Left
Addition Respects Formal-Difference Equivalence on the Right
Existence of an Iterator Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Forced Values Are Unique
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. If $(n,w_0)\in R^*$ and $(n,w_1)\in R^*$, then $w_0=w_1$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Consistency of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$. Then $R^*$ is an iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Midpoint Lies Strictly Between Rationals
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x<y \Longrightarrow x< (x+y)\cdot 2_{\mathbb{Q}}^{-1}<y. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Completeness of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there exists $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Determinism of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there is at most one $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Respects Fraction Equivalence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: If $[a,b]=[a',b']$ and $[c,d]=[c',d']$, then \[ [a,b]\cdot[c,d]=[a',b']\cdot[c',d']. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Respects Fraction Equivalence on the Left
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: If $[a,b]=[a',b']$, then \[ [a,b]\cdot[c,d]=[a',b']\cdot[c,d]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Respects Fraction Equivalence on the Right
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: If $[c,d]=[c',d']$, then \[ [a,b]\cdot[c,d]=[a,b]\cdot[c',d']. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Respects Formal-Difference Equivalence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: If $[a,b]=[a',b']$ and $[c,d]=[c',d']$, then \[ [a,b]\cdot[c,d]=[a',b']\cdot[c',d']. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Respects Formal-Difference Equivalence on the Left
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: If $[a,b]=[a',b']$, then \[ [a,b]\cdot[c,d]=[a',b']\cdot[c,d]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Respects Formal-Difference Equivalence on the Right
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: If $[c,d]=[c',d']$, then \[ [a,b]\cdot[c,d]=[a,b]\cdot[c',d']. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Nonzero Predicate Is Well-Defined on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every prefraction $(a,b)\in\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Q}$, \[ [a,b]\ne 0_{\mathbb{Q}} \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a\ne 0_{\mathbb{Z}}. \] Thus nonzeroness is independent of the representative.

transitive_dependencies.title: Reciprocal Lands in $\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: If $[a,b]\ne 0_{\mathbb{Q}}$, then $a\ne 0_{\mathbb{Z}}$, so $(b,a)$ is a prefraction.

transitive_dependencies.title: Two Is Nonzero in $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In $\mathbb{Q}$, \[ 2_{\mathbb{Q}}\ne 0_{\mathbb{Q}}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of General Recursive Functions
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=\Phi(n,h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Vanishing Criterion in $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every prefraction $(a,b)\in\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Q}$, \[ [a,b]=0_{\mathbb{Q}} \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a=0_{\mathbb{Z}}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Equality Criterion for Rational Classes
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For prefractions $(a,b),(c,d)\in\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Q}$, \[ [a,b]=[c,d] \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a\cdot d=b\cdot c. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Product of Positives Is Positive on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ 0_{\mathbb{Q}}<x\land 0_{\mathbb{Q}}<y \Longrightarrow 0_{\mathbb{Q}}<x\cdot y. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ (a+b)+c=a+(b+c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Is Commutative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ a+b=b+a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Cancellation for Addition
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ a+b=a+c \Longrightarrow b=c. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{Q}$ Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y,z\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ (x+y)+z=x+(y+z). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{Q}$ Is Commutative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x+y=y+x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{Q}$ Is Well-Defined
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Addition determines a well-defined binary operation $\mathbb{Q}\times\mathbb{Q}\to\mathbb{Q}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$ Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b,c\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ (a+_{\mathbb{W}}b)+_{\mathbb{W}}c = a+_{\mathbb{W}}(b+_{\mathbb{W}}c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$ Satisfies Cancellation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b,c\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=a+_{\mathbb{W}}c\Longrightarrow b=c. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$ Is Commutative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=b+_{\mathbb{W}}a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{Z}$ Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y,z\in\mathbb{Z}$, \[ (x+y)+z=x+(y+z). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{Z}$ Is Commutative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y\in\mathbb{Z}$, \[ x+y=y+x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{Z}$ Is Well-Defined
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Addition determines a well-defined binary operation $\mathbb{Z}\times\mathbb{Z}\to\mathbb{Z}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Preserves Order on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Addition preserves non-strict order on both sides in $\mathbb{Q}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Preserves Order on the Right
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ a \le b \Longrightarrow a+c \le b+c. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Preserves Strict Order on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Addition preserves strict order on both sides in $\mathbb{Q}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Successor on the Right
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m,n \in P$, \[ m+S(n)=S(m+n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition With $1$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m \in P$, \[ m+1=S(m). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Density of $\mathbb{Q}$ in Itself
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x<y \Longrightarrow \exists r\in\mathbb{Q}\ (x<r<y). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Every Element Is Either $1$ or a Successor
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $x \in P$, either $x=1$ or there exists $u \in P$ such that $S(u)=x$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Every Integer Has an Additive Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $x\in\mathbb{Z}$ there exists $y\in\mathbb{Z}$ such that \[ x+y=0_{\mathbb{Z}} \qquad\text{and}\qquad y+x=0_{\mathbb{Z}}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Every Nonzero Rational Has a Multiplicative Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $x\in\mathbb{Q}$ with $x\ne 0_{\mathbb{Q}}$, there exists $y\in\mathbb{Q}$ such that \[ x\cdot y=1_{\mathbb{Q}} \qquad\text{and}\qquad y\cdot x=1_{\mathbb{Q}}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Every Rational Has an Additive Inverse
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $x\in\mathbb{Q}$ there exists $y\in\mathbb{Q}$ such that \[ x+y=0_{\mathbb{Q}} \qquad\text{and}\qquad y+x=0_{\mathbb{Q}}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: General Recursion by State Encoding
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. There exists a function $H:P\to P\times W$ such that \[ H(1)=(1,c) \] and \[ \forall n\in P\; \bigl(H(S(n))=(S(\pi_1(H(n))),\Phi(\pi_1(H(n)),\pi_2(H(n))))\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: General Recursion Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $\Phi:P\times W\to W$ be a stage-dependent step rule. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=\Phi(n,f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Addition Preserves Order on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y,z\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x\le y \Longrightarrow z+x\le z+y. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Addition Preserves Strict Order on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y,z\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x<y \Longrightarrow z+x<z+y. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Distributivity on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y,z\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x\cdot(y+z)=x\cdot y+x\cdot z. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Distributivity on $\mathbb{Z}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y,z\in\mathbb{Z}$, \[ x\cdot(y+z)=x\cdot y+x\cdot z. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Multiplication by Positives Preserves Order on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y,z\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ 0_{\mathbb{Q}}<z\land x\le y \Longrightarrow z\cdot x\le z\cdot y. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Multiplication by Positives Preserves Strict Order on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y,z\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ 0_{\mathbb{Q}}<z\land x<y \Longrightarrow z\cdot x<z\cdot y. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ (a \cdot b) \cdot c = a \cdot (b \cdot c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Cancellation for Multiplication
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. Multiplication on $P$ satisfies cancellation on both sides: \[ a\cdot b=a\cdot c\Longrightarrow b=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad b\cdot a=c\cdot a\Longrightarrow b=c. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Is Commutative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ a \cdot b = b \cdot a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Distributes Over Addition
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ a \cdot (b+c) = (a \cdot b) + (a \cdot c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Distributes over Addition on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Multiplication distributes over addition on both sides in $\mathbb{Q}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Distributes over Addition on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b,c\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}(b+_{\mathbb{W}}c) = (a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}b)+_{\mathbb{W}}(a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Distributes over Addition on $\mathbb{Z}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Multiplication distributes over addition on both sides in $\mathbb{Z}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Left Cancellation for Multiplication
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ a\cdot b=a\cdot c \Longrightarrow b=c. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{Q}$ Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y,z\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ (x\cdot y)\cdot z=x\cdot(y\cdot z). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{Q}$ Is Commutative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x\cdot y=y\cdot x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{Q}$ Is Well-Defined
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Multiplication determines a well-defined binary operation $\mathbb{Q}\times\mathbb{Q}\to\mathbb{Q}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{W}$ Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b,c\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ (a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}b)\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}c = a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}(b\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{W}$ Is Commutative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}b=b\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{Z}$ Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y,z\in\mathbb{Z}$, \[ (x\cdot y)\cdot z=x\cdot(y\cdot z). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{Z}$ Is Commutative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y\in\mathbb{Z}$, \[ x\cdot y=y\cdot x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication on $\mathbb{Z}$ Is Well-Defined
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Multiplication determines a well-defined binary operation $\mathbb{Z}\times\mathbb{Z}\to\mathbb{Z}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Preserves and Reflects Strict Order
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $k,m,n \in P$, \[ m<n \Longleftrightarrow k\cdot m<k\cdot n. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Right Cancellation for Multiplication
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ b\cdot a=c\cdot a \Longrightarrow b=c. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication Successor on the Right
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m,n \in P$, \[ m \cdot S(n) = (m \cdot n) + m. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication With $1$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m \in P$, \[ m \cdot 1 = m. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Negation Gives a Left Additive Inverse on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $x\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ (-x)+x=0_{\mathbb{Q}}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Negation Gives a Left Additive Inverse on $\mathbb{Z}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $x\in\mathbb{Z}$, \[ (-x)+x=0_{\mathbb{Z}}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Negation on $\mathbb{Q}$ Is Well-Defined
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Negation determines a well-defined unary operation $\mathbb{Q}\to\mathbb{Q}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Negation on $\mathbb{Z}$ Is Well-Defined
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Negation determines a well-defined unary operation $\mathbb{Z}\to\mathbb{Z}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: No Object Is Its Own Successor
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $n \in P$, \[ S(n) \neq n. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One Is a Left Multiplicative Identity on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $x\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ 1_{\mathbb{Q}}\cdot x=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One Is a Left Multiplicative Identity on $\mathbb{Z}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $x\in\mathbb{Z}$, \[ 1_{\mathbb{Z}}\cdot x=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One Is a Multiplicative Identity on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $x\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ 1_{\mathbb{Q}}\cdot x=x \qquad\text{and}\qquad x\cdot 1_{\mathbb{Q}}=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One Is a Multiplicative Identity on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $a\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ 1\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}a=a \qquad\text{and}\qquad a\cdot_{\mathbb{W}}1=a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One Is a Multiplicative Identity on $\mathbb{Z}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $x\in\mathbb{Z}$, \[ 1_{\mathbb{Z}}\cdot x=x \qquad\text{and}\qquad x\cdot 1_{\mathbb{Z}}=x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One Plus $n$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $n \in P$, \[ 1+n=S(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One Times $n$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $n \in P$, \[ 1 \cdot n = n. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Order Is Antisymmetric on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ \bigl(a \le b \land b \le a\bigr) \Longrightarrow a=b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Order on $\mathbb{Q}$ Is Antisymmetric
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x\le y\land y\le x \Longrightarrow x=y. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Order on $\mathbb{Q}$ Is Reflexive
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $x\in\mathbb{Q}$, $x\le x$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Order on $\mathbb{Q}$ Is Total
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x\le y\text{ or }y\le x. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Order on $\mathbb{Q}$ Is Transitive
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y,z\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x\le y\land y\le z \Longrightarrow x\le z. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Order on $\mathbb{W}$ Is Total
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b\in\mathbb{W}$, \[ a\leq_{\mathbb{W}}b \quad\text{or}\quad b\leq_{\mathbb{W}}a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Order Is Reflexive on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a \in P$, \[ a \le a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Order Is Transitive on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ \bigl(a \le b \land b \le c\bigr) \Longrightarrow a \le c. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Field One Is Positive
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an ordered field, \[ 0<1. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Field Squares Are Nonnegative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: In an ordered field, \[ 0\le a^2. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Multiplication by Positives Preserves Order on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Multiplication by a positive rational preserves non-strict order on both sides.

transitive_dependencies.title: $\mathbb{Q}$ Is an Additive Abelian Group
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The structure $(\mathbb{Q},+,0_{\mathbb{Q}})$ is an abelian group.

transitive_dependencies.title: $\mathbb{Q}$ Is a Field
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The structure $(\mathbb{Q},+,\cdot,0_{\mathbb{Q}},1_{\mathbb{Q}})$ is a field.

transitive_dependencies.title: $\mathbb{Q}$ Is an Ordered Field
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The structure \[ (\mathbb{Q},+,\cdot,0_{\mathbb{Q}},1_{\mathbb{Q}},\le) \] is an ordered field.

transitive_dependencies.title: Nonzero $\mathbb{Q}$ Is a Multiplicative Abelian Group
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The structure $(\mathbb{Q}\setminus\{0_{\mathbb{Q}}\},\cdot,1_{\mathbb{Q}})$ is an abelian group.

transitive_dependencies.title: $\mathbb{Q}$ Is a Totally Ordered Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The ordered pair $(\mathbb{Q},\le)$ is a totally ordered set.

transitive_dependencies.title: Reciprocal Gives a Left Multiplicative Inverse on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every nonzero $x\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x^{-1}\cdot x=1_{\mathbb{Q}}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Reciprocal on $\mathbb{Q}$ Is Well-Defined
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Reciprocal determines a well-defined map \[ \mathbb{Q}\setminus\{0_{\mathbb{Q}}\}\to\mathbb{Q}\setminus\{0_{\mathbb{Q}}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Sign Trichotomy on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For every $x\in\mathbb{Q}$, exactly one of \[ x<0_{\mathbb{Q}},\qquad x=0_{\mathbb{Q}},\qquad 0_{\mathbb{Q}}<x \] holds.

transitive_dependencies.title: Strict Order on $\mathbb{Q}$ Is Asymmetric
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x<y \Longrightarrow \neg(y<x). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Strict Order on $\mathbb{Q}$ Is Transitive
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y,z\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x<y\land y<z \Longrightarrow x<z. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Strict Order Successor Characterization
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ a<b \Longleftrightarrow S(a) \le b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Trichotomy on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, exactly one of the following holds: \[ a<b,\qquad a=b,\qquad b<a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Trichotomy on $\mathbb{Q}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y\in\mathbb{Q}$, exactly one of \[ x<y,\qquad x=y,\qquad y<x \] holds.

transitive_dependencies.title: $\mathbb{Z}$ Is an Additive Abelian Group
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The structure $(\mathbb{Z},+,0_{\mathbb{Z}})$ is an abelian group.

transitive_dependencies.title: $\mathbb{Z}$ Is a Commutative Ring
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The structure $(\mathbb{Z},+,\cdot,0_{\mathbb{Z}},1_{\mathbb{Z}})$ is a commutative ring.

transitive_dependencies.title: $\mathbb{Z}$ Has No Zero Divisors

transitive_dependencies.title: $\mathbb{Z}$ Is a Multiplicative Commutative Monoid
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The structure $(\mathbb{Z},\cdot,1_{\mathbb{Z}})$ is a commutative monoid.

transitive_dependencies.title: Zero Is Absorbing for Multiplication on $\mathbb{W}$

transitive_dependencies.title: Zero Is an Additive Identity on $\mathbb{Q}$

transitive_dependencies.title: Zero Is an Additive Identity on $\mathbb{W}$

transitive_dependencies.title: Zero Is an Additive Identity on $\mathbb{Z}$

transitive_dependencies.title: Zero Is a Left Additive Identity on $\mathbb{Q}$

transitive_dependencies.title: Zero Is a Left Additive Identity on $\mathbb{Z}$

dependencies.statement_preview: A rational number $[a,b]\in\mathbb{Q}$ is \emph{positive} if \[ a\cdot b>0_{\mathbb{Z}}. \]

dependencies.title: Subtraction on $\mathbb{Q}$
dependencies.statement_preview: For $x,y\in\mathbb{Q}$, define \[ x-y:=x+(-y). \]

dependencies.title: Midpoint Lies Strictly Between Rationals
dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y\in\mathbb{Q}$, \[ x<y \Longrightarrow x< (x+y)\cdot 2_{\mathbb{Q}}^{-1}<y. \]

dependencies.title: Two Is Nonzero in $\mathbb{Q}$
dependencies.statement_preview: In $\mathbb{Q}$, \[ 2_{\mathbb{Q}}\ne 0_{\mathbb{Q}}. \]

dependencies.title: One Is Positive in $\mathbb{Q}$
dependencies.statement_preview: In $\mathbb{Q}$, \[ 0_{\mathbb{Q}}<1_{\mathbb{Q}}. \]

dependencies.title: Trichotomy on $\mathbb{Q}$
dependencies.statement_preview: For all $x,y\in\mathbb{Q}$, exactly one of \[ x<y,\qquad x=y,\qquad y<x \] holds.

dependencies.title: $\mathbb{Q}$ Is an Ordered Field
dependencies.statement_preview: The structure \[ (\mathbb{Q},+,\cdot,0_{\mathbb{Q}},1_{\mathbb{Q}},\le) \] is an ordered field.

dependencies.title: Addition Preserves Order on $\mathbb{Q}$
dependencies.statement_preview: Addition preserves non-strict order on both sides in $\mathbb{Q}$.

dependencies.title: Multiplication by Positives Preserves Order on $\mathbb{Q}$
dependencies.statement_preview: Multiplication by a positive rational preserves non-strict order on both sides.

dependencies.title: $\mathbb{Q}$ Is a Field
dependencies.statement_preview: The structure $(\mathbb{Q},+,\cdot,0_{\mathbb{Q}},1_{\mathbb{Q}})$ is a field.

dependencies.title: $\mathbb{Q}$ Is a Totally Ordered Set
dependencies.statement_preview: The ordered pair $(\mathbb{Q},\le)$ is a totally ordered set.
